_metadata.description: Routing matrix defining how payment events are classified, routed to compliance teams, and what downstream actions are triggered. This matrix is for UI display and documentation. The authoritative routing logic is in agent/nodes.py (routing_decision_node) using Python constants. The LLM cannot modify routing targets.

BSA_COMPLIANCE.description: Bank Secrecy Act / Anti-Money Laundering Compliance Team
BSA_COMPLIANCE.responsibilities: OFAC holds, SAR filing decisions, CTR filing, high-risk country wires, PEP reviews
BSA_COMPLIANCE.escalation_path: BSA Officer → Chief Compliance Officer → Regulators (OFAC/FinCEN)
triggers: high_risk_country_flag = true AND payment_type IN [WIRE_INTERNATIONAL, WIRE_DOMESTIC, FEDWIRE]

DISPUTES.description: Consumer Disputes and Regulation E Team
DISPUTES.responsibilities: Reg E investigations, unauthorized ACH returns, provisional credit, customer notices
triggers: reg_e_applicable = true AND dispute_type IS NOT NULL
return_code IN [R07, R10, R11, R29, R33, R37, R38, R51]

FRAUD_OPERATIONS.responsibilities: BEC fraud investigations, wire fraud, account takeover, coordinating with law enforcement
FRAUD_OPERATIONS.escalation_path: Fraud Analyst → Fraud Manager → Law Enforcement Liaison

LEGAL.responsibilities: Late return disputes (potential Nacha rule violations), UCC Article 4A claims, litigation support
LEGAL.escalation_path: Legal Counsel → General Counsel → Outside Counsel
triggers: OFAC violation with potential IEEPA penalty exposure

PAYMENTS_OPS.responsibilities: NOC processing, administrative returns, format corrections, routine processing
PAYMENTS_OPS.escalation_path: Payments Ops Analyst → Payments Ops Manager
triggers: return_code IN [C01, C02, C03, C04, C05, C06, C07, C08, C09] (NOC codes)
return_code IN [R01, R02, R03, R04, R13, R17] (administrative returns)

AUTO_RESOLVE.description: Automated resolution — no human review required
AUTO_RESOLVE.responsibilities: Low-risk administrative events that can be processed without human involvement
triggers: No Reg E, OFAC, SAR, or unauthorized return flags

INVESTIGATE_AND_RETURN.description: Conduct full Reg E investigation and initiate unauthorized ACH return if evidence supports

OFAC_HOLD.description: Block transaction immediately. File OFAC report within 10 business days. Consider SAR.

FRAUD_INVESTIGATION.description: Initiate fraud investigation. Contact receiving institution for recall. Advise customer to file IC3 complaint.

NOC_UPDATE_REQUIRED.description: Notify originator of NOC correction required. Must update within 6 banking days.

DENY_CLAIM.description: Dispute claim denied after investigation. Reverse provisional credit if issued. Send Reg E denial notice.

APPROVE_CLAIM.description: Dispute claim approved. Make provisional credit permanent. Send confirmation notice.

ADMINISTRATIVE_RETURN.description: Process return entry for administrative reason (NSF, account closed, etc.)

ESCALATE_TO_LEGAL.description: Escalate to Legal team for late return dispute or UCC 4A liability review

hitl_triggers.description: Conditions that always require human review before any action is taken

always_hitl_events.reason: Federal regulations require human authorization before any action on a sanctioned entity transaction
Wire fraud requires human investigation; UCC Article 4A customer rights must be evaluated
BSA requires that a qualified BSA officer, not an automated system, make SAR filing determinations
CTR filing is a legal obligation. Human verification required before filing.
FATF guidance and correspondent banking policy require human oversight for high-risk jurisdiction transactions
always_hitl_events.regulatory_basis: FATF Recommendations R.10-R.13, correspondent banking AML policy

always_hitl_events.reason: Late returns may constitute Nacha rule violations exposing institution to fines. Legal review required.
always_hitl_events.regulatory_basis: Nacha OR Section 2.12.2, potential Rule 10000 violation

threshold_hitl.reason: SR 11-7 model governance requires human oversight for high-risk automated decisions
SLA breaches or near-breaches require escalated human attention
Consumer protection right — documented human review required per Reg E

threshold_hitl.trigger: reg_e_applicable = true AND dispute_type IS NOT NULL
threshold_hitl.reason: Reg E investigation requires an accountable human reviewer

APPROVE_RESOLUTION.description: Reviewer confirms the automated resolution recommendation is correct
APPROVE_RESOLUTION.next_step: resolution_drafting — draft customer notice and internal memo

OVERRIDE_RESOLUTION.description: Reviewer provides a different resolution type than the automated recommendation

ESCALATE.description: Reviewer sends to a higher authority (Legal, BSA Officer, etc.)
ESCALATE.next_step: audit_finalize — record escalation destination, no automated drafting

REJECT_CLAIM.description: Reviewer determines the dispute claim is invalid
REJECT_CLAIM.next_step: audit_finalize — record rejection basis, denial notice drafted separately

CRITICAL.description: Immediate action required. Mandatory HITL. OFAC hit is a hard override to CRITICAL.

HIGH.description: Elevated risk. Human review required. Action within 24 hours.
examples: Strong Reg E dispute with large provisional credit

MEDIUM.description: Moderate risk. Human review recommended. Standard SLA applies.

LOW.description: Low risk. May auto-resolve for administrative events. Human review optional.
